title: Capture original idea, intended users, product boundaries, and explicit non-goals
description: Capture original idea, intended users, product boundaries, and explicit non-goals

title: Record user Q&A intake decisions before execution
description: Capture worker, target platform, repo/module location, scope, owner/reviewer/exit authority, and unresolved route assumptions.

title: Resolve worker execution topology for lulu and HASHI9 Windows
description: Decide whether lulu works from HASHI1 and deploys/tests on HASHI9, or whether a lulu worker must be active on HASHI9.

title: Confirm evidence-based automatic exit policy
description: Define objective evidence that lets Zelda exit automatically without user approval: tests, smoke results, Akane reviews, docs, and no blockers.

title: Extract requirements and architecture constraints from Watchtower discussion records
description: Extract requirements and architecture constraints from Watchtower discussion records

title: Design minimal core with modular sidecar boundaries, narrow rescue API, and structured audit logging
description: Design minimal core with modular sidecar boundaries, narrow rescue API, and structured audit logging

title: Model explicit Windows, WSL, and remote target handling without guessing paths or runtime context
description: Model explicit Windows, WSL, and remote target handling without guessing paths or runtime context

title: Break implementation into product phases, taskboard items, waits, issues, artifacts, and evidence checkpoints
description: Break implementation into product phases, taskboard items, waits, issues, artifacts, and evidence checkpoints

title: Send plan and architecture package to akane for independent plan review
description: Use /hchat akane as the default independent reviewer; include goal, boundaries, non-goals, architecture, WSL/Windows handling, API surface, risks, and proposed acceptance tests.

title: Wait for akane plan review comments and record them as issues/tasks
description: Do not proceed to implementation until review comments are captured, classified, and represented in the taskboard or issue register.

title: Address plan review comments with update -> review-fix -> recheck loop
description: For each actionable plan comment, update the plan/design, add tests or constraints when needed, and keep looping until the concern is resolved or explicitly accepted.

title: Record plan review clearance before implementation starts
description: Implementation can start only after akane has no blocking plan concerns or blockers are explicitly documented and accepted.

title: Implement the smallest vertical slice that proves the end-to-end Watchtower flow
description: Implement the smallest vertical slice that proves the end-to-end Watchtower flow

title: Run internal test loop: test -> bug found -> debug -> fix -> retest until green
description: Run internal test loop: test -> bug found -> debug -> fix -> retest until green

title: Run real end-to-end smoke test and prepare for safe live acceptance in the intended target environment
description: Smoke is not sufficient for exit. It must be followed by safe live acceptance when the product has real runtime, network, rescue, persistence, or user-visible behavior.

title: Define safe live-test preflight before destructive or real-world actions
description: Identify protected systems, allowed targets, exact processes/services, independent control channel, auth requirements, dynamic ports, rollback plan, and evidence to collect before live testing starts.
notes: Added after live Watchtower rescue testing showed this must be a standard auto vibe coding gate.

title: Run real live acceptance through the intended external/user path
description: Exercise the deployed product in the real environment as an external/user would, not through a local bypass or mock-only path. Include controlled failure/recovery or off/on behavior when that is part of the product promise.
notes: Added after live Watchtower rescue testing showed this must be a standard auto vibe coding gate.

title: Verify live behavior from all required views and directions
description: Check status endpoints, list views, LAN/WSL/Windows perspectives, audit logs, process health, and user-visible behavior after live actions.
notes: Added after live Watchtower rescue testing showed this must be a standard auto vibe coding gate.

title: Loop on live failures until the real product works
description: If live testing fails: restore service first, record failure evidence, fix root cause, rerun focused automated tests, rerun live acceptance, and repeat until green before exit.
notes: Added after live Watchtower rescue testing showed this must be a standard auto vibe coding gate.

title: Collect acceptance evidence: logs, test results, operator docs, and known limitations
description: Collect acceptance evidence: logs, test results, operator docs, and known limitations

title: Send completed product and evidence package to akane for independent final product review
description: Include implementation summary, test results, real smoke evidence, logs, docs, known limitations, and exit criteria status.

title: Wait for final product review comments and convert actionable feedback into issues/tasks
description: Every actionable final review comment must become an issue or task with owner, evidence, and retest requirement.

title: Address final review comments through test -> bug -> debug -> fix -> retest loops
description: Fix all blocking review comments and rerun relevant automated and end-to-end tests until evidence is green.

title: Record final review clearance or documented no-blocker state before exit
description: The superloop may exit only after final review comments are addressed and no blocking concern remains.

title: Exit only after the whole idea is built, documented, and proven functional by automated tests plus safe live acceptance evidence
description: No exit on tests, smoke, or review alone when live behavior is part of the product promise. Exit requires objective live evidence or a documented, accepted reason live testing is impossible.
